success_surface: Conditional cell extraction based on visual formatting, multi-sheet data transfer with style preservation, and comprehensive verification
effective_behaviors: Use openpyxl instead of pandas when cell formatting needs to be preserved or detected
Iterate through cell ranges to detect conditional formatting before extracting data
Handle multiple variations of color codes when checking cell fill styles
Clear target destination cells before writing new values to prevent data contamination
Apply consistent formatting to transferred cells to maintain visual coherence
Verify output by reloading the saved file to confirm changes persisted correctly
Log each execution step with progress markers for traceability
Save to temporary path first, then validate before finalizing
evidence: openpyxl.load_workbook(..., data_only=False) used to preserve formatting information
Color detection loop checked three RGB format variations (FFFF00, 00FFFF00, FFFFFF00)
Target range in master sheet cleared before population (STEP 2)
PatternFill and Border styles explicitly applied to transferred cells
Output file reloaded and verified after saving to confirm correctness

memory_items.title: Format-Aware Cell Detection
memory_items.lesson: When extracting cells based on visual properties like color, use openpyxl and check multiple color code variations since Excel may store colors differently
memory_items.generalization: Always validate fill.start_color.rgb against common hex variations; handle None cases gracefully; prefer PatternFill class for consistency

memory_items.title: Destination Range Hygiene
memory_items.lesson: Before populating a target range with new data, clear existing content to prevent stale values from remaining in cells beyond the new dataset
memory_items.generalization: Calculate max needed columns dynamically; iterate through old range and set value=None before writing new values

memory_items.title: Style Preservation During Transfer
memory_items.lesson: When moving data between sheets, replicate source formatting explicitly rather than assuming it carries over
memory_items.generalization: Define Style objects (fill, border, alignment) once and apply consistently across all transferred cells; use classes not shortcuts

memory_items.title: Two-Phase Verification Workflow
memory_items.lesson: After modifying and saving a workbook, reload it fresh to verify persistence of all changes before considering the task complete
memory_items.generalization: Load output file into new Workbook instance; spot-check critical cells for both data values and formatting; log verification summary

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Visual Property-Based Selection
- Use `openpyxl` when filtering or extracting cells based on formatting attributes (color, font, borders)
- Check multiple RGB color format variations (`FFFF00`, `00FFFF00`, `FFFFFF00`) as Excel stores them inconsistently
- Guard against `None` or missing fill attributes to avoid AttributeError exceptions

### Target Range Management
- Clear destination range completely before populating with new values to avoid ghost data
- Dynamically calculate required range size rather than hardcoding column limits
- Reset both cell values and any inherited formatting from previous content

### Consistent Styling Application
- Define Style objects (PatternFill, Border, Side, Alignment) once and reuse across batch operations
- Apply full cell styling (value + fill + border + alignment) to each cell individually
- Do not rely on default inheritance; explicit is more reliable

### Validation Protocol
- Always reload saved output file for post-write verification
- Verify both data integrity AND formatting preservation in verification phase
- Log verification metrics (count, coordinate list, sample values) for audit trail

### Execution Transparency
- Print progress markers at each major step boundary
- Report counts at key stages (cells found, cleared, transferred, verified)
- Maintain clear section headers in output logs for readability
